extra.message: User input from request.args.get('name') is rendered with '| safe' filter, disabling Jinja2 auto-escaping. Allows reflected XSS via the 'name' query parameter (e.g., ?name=<script>alert(1)</script>). Source: app/blueprints/sandboxes/views.py:13.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: Cookie value from request.cookies.get('name') and form value from request.form.get('name') are rendered with '| safe' filter, disabling Jinja2 auto-escaping. Allows both stored (via cookie persistence) and reflected XSS. Source: app/blueprints/sandboxes/views.py:27-31.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-supplied 'from_user' field from Message model is rendered with '| safe' filter in the admin messages view. An attacker can inject XSS payloads via the unauthenticated API endpoint MessagesView.post() (app/blueprints/api/views.py:43-56) or via the authenticated feedback form (app/blueprints/account_area/views.py:61-78). The payload is stored in the database and executed when an admin views the messages page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: User-supplied 'message' field from Message model is rendered with '| safe' filter in the admin messages view. An attacker can inject XSS payloads via the unauthenticated API endpoint MessagesView.post() (app/blueprints/api/views.py:43-56) or via the authenticated feedback form (app/blueprints/account_area/views.py:61-78). The payload is stored in the database and executed when an admin views the messages page.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation (Cross-site Scripting)

extra.message: The 'next' parameter from request.args is passed directly to flask's redirect() without any validation. An attacker can craft a URL like /toggle_xss_protection/?next=https://evil.com to redirect users to arbitrary external sites. This enables phishing and other attacks.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site (Open Redirect)

extra.message: Passwords are stored as plaintext strings in the database (password = db.Column(db.String(200))). No password hashing is used. Passwords are compared in plaintext at app/blueprints/login/views.py:17 (user.password == password) and stored in plaintext at app/blueprints/account_area/views.py:34-36. This means all passwords would be exposed in a database breach.

cwe: CWE-256: Plaintext Storage of a Password

extra.message: User.serialize property includes the plaintext password field in its JSON output. This is used at app/blueprints/api/views.py:72 to return all users' passwords via the API endpoint /api/v1/UsersView/. Any admin accessing this endpoint would receive all user passwords in plaintext.

cwe: CWE-359: Exposure of Private Personal Information to an Unauthorized Actor

extra.message: UsersView.get() passes a raw SQLAlchemy User ORM object to jsonify (ret = {'user': user}) instead of user.serialize. This causes a TypeError/500 error, and with DEBUG=True (set in app/flask_xss.py:26), the error page exposes a Python traceback with file paths, code snippets, and database URI, leaking sensitive internal application details to users.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: The Flask SECRET_KEY is hardcoded as a literal string 'Keep it secret, keep it safe' in the application source code. This key is used for session signing and CSRF token generation. If the source code is ever exposed (e.g., via version control), attackers can forge session cookies and impersonate users.

Flask's DEBUG mode is enabled (app.config['DEBUG'] = True). In production, this exposes the interactive Werkzeug debugger to remote users, allowing arbitrary code execution on the server through the debugger console. It also leaks detailed error traces with code snippets and environment information.

The 'add_admin' management command has a default password of 'admin' (def add_admin(password='admin')). If an operator runs the command without specifying a password override, an admin account with well-known credentials (admin/admin) is created, enabling unauthorized administrative access.

MessagesView.post() has no authentication or authorization decorator (no @login_required or @admin_required). Any unauthenticated user can hit the API endpoint and create arbitrary message entries in the database. Combined with the stored XSS in admin/message.html, this enables unauthenticated stored XSS attacks.
